purpose: Collect current primary-source patterns for a report-only CEO/manager inbox packet layer after ceo_operator_event_surface_contract_v1.

sources.name: LangGraph interrupts
sources.observed_pattern: Dynamic interrupts surface JSON-serializable payloads, persist graph state, and resume by passing an explicit command against a thread/checkpoint pointer.
sources.local_design_implication: Inbox packets should include a local correlation id, source event type, source artifact path, and resume/decision state as data only. Do not start an interrupt transport.

sources.name: LangChain human-in-the-loop middleware
sources.observed_pattern: Tool calls can be interrupted by policy and answered through approve, edit, reject, or respond decision modes.
sources.local_design_implication: The local packet should separate proposed action details from permitted decision types, and every decision mode should remain a non-executing preview until a later signed decision route exists.

sources.name: LangChain Agent Inbox
sources.observed_pattern: Agent Inbox uses an action request, per-interrupt allow flags, and human response types for human-in-the-loop agents.
sources.local_design_implication: Adopt action_request plus allowed_decision_types, but store only local report packets instead of connecting to a LangGraph deployment.

sources.name: Temporal workflow message passing
sources.observed_pattern: Temporal distinguishes read-only Queries, asynchronous write Signals, and tracked synchronous Updates.
sources.local_design_implication: Inbox packets should classify the requested operator attention as read_only_notice, async_decision_request, or tracked_decision_request without performing any write.

sources.name: Inngest human-in-the-loop workflows
sources.observed_pattern: Human approval flows use durable waits for a correlated approval response, timeout handling, and explicit approved/rejected/timed-out branches.
sources.local_design_implication: Inbox packets should require correlation_id, timeout_policy, stale_after_utc, and branch previews for accept, reject, timeout, and no-op.

sources.name: HumanLayer CodeLayer
sources.observed_pattern: The repository positions CodeLayer as an open-source IDE for orchestrating parallel coding agents and human-guided workflows.
sources.local_design_implication: The CEO inbox should keep owner thread, lane, agent, evidence, and handoff fields visible so humans can supervise parallel agents without direct agent messaging.

sources.name: Async Human Approval for AI Agents
sources.observed_pattern: The project emphasizes async approval waits, reminders, escalation, and timeout behavior for blocked agents.
sources.local_design_implication: Use reminder/escalation fields only as local previews; do not schedule reminders or send notifications from the first packet contract.

hard_denials: No operator event emission or persistence.
No service-request assignment, approval, rejection, start, update, or completion.
No worker, browser, runtime, MCP, or model/API start/call.
No public, account, wallet, payment, security-testing, legal, or external side effect.

next_action: Build ceo_manager_inbox_packet_v1 as a local report-only schema and validator only after explicit design approval; keep all transport, mutation, approval, worker, browser, model/MCP, public, account, wallet, payment, and external actions disabled.
